item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quirky and enjoyable. This captures the element of mystery in more ways than one.

I noticed that "next to" was repeated in the first Artifacts entry. Possible typo, thought I would let you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, that's all I can rightfully say :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this SCP, except for sharing the concerns voiced by peppersghost. My only other takeaway is that it might be improved with the incorporation of more of Christie's life/works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I feel like this type of concept works better with the Rogetpost and SCP-3780, enough so that this article just feels like treading old ground.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was already in firm "meh" territory for me, and when I got to the part where it starts affecting the Foundation, it tipped things into the downvote zone. Having the SCP affect the Foundation as a stinger ending is rather trite at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doesn't go anywhere unexpected, but putting Agatha Christie in this contest and managing to get away with it earns my +1 on that basis alone.

Also, very nice SCP number choice. That's the first time I've said that about an article but it really ties in well with your subject matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have read the Rogetpost, but again I think this isn't so much "Christie is killed by her own works" so much as "Christie is used as a plot device (as all her murder victims were) in a series of events which are both classic murder mystery setups as well as part of a grander, more modern and less classic mystery in their own right." It's not about the specific content of Christie, but rather her style and the influence it has had on mystery and suspense, and features both a straight usage and subversion of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moreover, I don't think this is so much about alternate fates as it is metacommentary on the nature of Christie's work.

Oh! There happens to be another SCP wherein author Roget's author avatar gets killed off by his own SCPs or something to that effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not aware of that one!

Moreover, I don't think this is so much about alternate fates as it is metacommentary on the nature of Christie's work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I daresay this is quite reminiscent of that other SCP with JFK's death in it (an alternate fate for a famous person, and devoting the article to show all those ways of alternate takes).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hammered this out in like an hour and coldposted it in a flash of inspiration

Normally I would never do that but it seemed to fit the spirit of the contest so there we go
